parameters.name: setting
parameters.description: The configuration setting to modify

parameters.name: device
parameters.description: The device where configuration is applied

notes: Examples consistently refer to configuring CODEC settings on PABX equipment.

parameters.name: selection
parameters.description: The item to select or choose

notes: Examples show selection/choice actions but don't specify context (e.g., dialing, settings). Could map to various actions like dial_call or configure_settings depending on broader test context.

parameters.name: pin
parameters.description: The password or PIN to enter

notes: All examples specify entering an incorrect password, which maps to the 'enter_pin' action. The parameter is named 'pin' to maintain consistency with other authentication-related actions, though the examples refer to it as a password.

parameters.name: location
parameters.description: Location on the page to be selected

representative_sentence: Choose a {location} on the page and validate the selection.
notes: Examples describe selecting and validating a location on a page, which doesn't match any predefined VoIP actions. This appears to be a UI interaction rather than a call control or device management action.

parameters.name: emplacement
parameters.description: Location or position to select on the page

representative_sentence: Choose {emplacement} in the page and validate your choice.
notes: Examples describe a UI interaction involving selection and validation, but don't clearly map to any specific VoIP action from the allowed list. The action appears to be part of a configuration or setup flow rather than a core call control function.

parameters.name: source_device
parameters.description: The device initiating the call (set under evaluation)

parameters.name: target_device
parameters.description: The device receiving the call (distant set)

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: Communication mode for the source device

representative_sentence: Initiate a communication between {source_device} and {target_device} using {mode}.
notes: Examples describe initiating communication between two specified devices with a required mode on the source device. This aligns with dial_call action for VoIP test automation.

parameters.name: device
parameters.description: Target device(s) to hang up call on

parameters.name: action_target
parameters.description: The target to cancel

parameters.name: duration
parameters.description: Optional call duration before hangup

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: Minimum software version required for the feature

parameters.name: capability
parameters.description: Feature capability that must be allowed by server

parameters.name: user_type
parameters.description: Type of user account

parameters.name: device_state
parameters.description: Required state of the SIP phone
parameters.example: in a call with another phone

parameters.name: call_scenario
parameters.description: Call scenario to be executed
parameters.example: other phone makes a second call to a different phone

representative_sentence: From version {version}, if {capability} is allowed by OXE server for {user_type}, {action_type} or ensure {device_state} and {call_scenario}.
notes: Examples describe two different scenarios: 1) Activating interphony feature via settings/progkey, 2) Setting up a specific call scenario. No single clear action matches both examples from the allowed list.
